g.text: result | — | — | root can tune; world can observe
0 | no sysfs file created | load-time only, invisible
0444 | world-readable, read-only | observe but never change
0644 | root can write | tunable by root at runtime
DANGER — 0666 lets ANY user modify kernel state
other-write | w | unprivileged user can echo new value
attack | — | user sets buffer_size=0 → crash on next kmalloc
fix | — | NEVER use 0666 for module parameters
rule | — | use 0444 unless root-tunable is explicitly needed
